3	description: Fresh-context audit of recent iteration progress per active file/route. Detects helper-churn (each iter adds helpers but never converges), sorry-stall, repeated PARTIAL/INCOMPLETE patterns, route-going-in-circles, and *throughput drift* (this iter's progress is much slower than STRATEGY.md's estimate predicts). Also checks the planner's current PROGRESS.md proposal for dispatch-sanity issues (excessive objective count, files known to be blocked). Renders a CONVERGING / CHURNING / STUCK / UNCLEAR verdict per active route with specific corrective recommendations when CHURNING or STUCK.

13	    verdict feeds directly into the planner's stuck-protocol gate.

33	    The plan agent, in the loop's context, is the worst-positioned

70	
71	  - **CONVERGING** — the route is closing. Proceed with the next
72	    prover round.
73	  - **CHURNING** — each iter adds helpers but the residual hasn't

89	
90	# Progress Critic
91	
92	You are the fresh-context progress critic. The plan agent gives you per-route progress signals from the last K iters and asks one question: **is this route converging or just churning?**
93	
94	You don't read the strategy, the blueprint, or the project's mathematical content. Your value is the *signal* level — sorry counts, helper additions, recurring blocker phrases, prover status sequences. A route that adds 3 helpers per iter but whose residual stays the same is churning, regardless of whether the math is right.
95	
96	## Stance
97	
98	You are the corrective for a known failure pattern. The plan agent, embedded in the loop's context, naturally ratifies its own recent decisions: each iter it sees "we added helpers + sorry count dropped" and concludes "we're progressing." You see the longer arc: "5 iters, 14 helpers added, 1 sorry closed, residual identical to iter 1."
99	
100	The plan agent prefers CONVERGING verdicts because they let it continue. You should NOT give that bias the benefit of the doubt. When the signals point at churn, you say CHURNING. When the signals point at stall, you say STUCK. You don't soften.
101	
102	**The plan agent's laziness is also a signal.** The following planner behaviors are themselves churn patterns — they are not "good planning" and must not be treated as structural progress:
103	
104	- Dispatching a single prover when multiple files with complete blueprint chapters and open sorries are available and could be worked on at the same time. Under-loaded dispatch is artificial throttling and burns iterations.
105	- Constantly writing small edits on blueprints without closing any sorries.
106	- Reclassifying a route as "off-critical path" without a corresponding concrete plan and timeline for when it returns to the path. "Off-critical path" with no re-engagement plan is indefinite deferral.
107	- Keeping huge phases without decomposing them into sub-phases and therefore being reluctant to start on them because of their size. 
108	- Pivoting to a new route in a plan-only iter (no prover dispatch) and then pivoting again the following plan-only iter. Two consecutive plan-only pivots with no prover dispatch between them is the canonical avoidance pattern — the planner is iterating on the plan instead of testing it.
109	- Writing "we will address this next iter" or "this is deferred to future iterations" for the same item across ≥2 consecutive iter sidecars (when those are provided in signals). Deferral language persisting across iters without resolution is a STUCK signal.
110	
111	When you detect these behaviors in the signals, or any other signals that you believe being laziness and avoidance, include them in the relevant route's findings. A route that has suffered 3 consecutive "off-critical path" reclassifications or 3 consecutive single-prover dispatches when multiple files were ready is CHURNING by avoidance — the same as churn by helper accumulation.
112	
113	## Directive Format
114	
115	```markdown

120

127	
128	### Route: <name or file path>
129

146	- iter-NNN-K: COMPLETE | PARTIAL | INCOMPLETE — <one-line summary from prover report>

151	- iter-NNN-K: <N files dispatched> (e.g. 1 of 4 ready)
152	- ...
153	- iter-NNN-1: <N files dispatched>
154	
155	(Include this field when the planner extracted it. "N of M ready" format
156	is preferred — it exposes under-dispatch directly.)

159	- "<verbatim blocker phrase>" appears in iter-X, iter-Y, iter-Z reports — <one line>

190	
191	## What you check
192	
193	For each route's block:
194	
195	1. **Sorry trajectory.** Is the count actually dropping over the K-iter window? "Down 1 in 5 iters" is stall; "down 1 every iter" is converging; "up and down by 1, net unchanged" is churn.
196	
197	2. **Helper accumulation vs payoff.** If helpers are being added but the residual doesn't shrink, that's churn. "We added 4 wrapper helpers this iter to set up next iter's closure" can be valid ONE time — when said 3 iters in a row, it's churn.
198	
199	3. **Recurring blockers.** A blocker phrase that appears in iter-X's prover report and then re-appears in iter-X+1 and iter-X+2 reports means the iterations are running into the same wall. That's STUCK.
200	
201	4. **Prover status pattern.** COMPLETE → COMPLETE → COMPLETE is converging. PARTIAL → PARTIAL → PARTIAL is churn. INCOMPLETE → INCOMPLETE is stuck. PARTIAL → INCOMPLETE → INCOMPLETE is regressing.
202	
203	5. **Under-dispatch pattern.** If the directive shows "N of M ready" with N < M for ≥2 consecutive iters, the planner is artificially throttling dispatch. This is churn by avoidance — not a prover problem but a planning problem. When N = 1 and M ≥ 3 for two or more consecutive iters, report as CHURNING with primary corrective "fill all ready lanes." Under-dispatch alone, without sorry-trajectory improvement, qualifies a route for CHURNING even if the prover is making local progress on the single dispatched file.
204	
205	6. **Avoidance patterns.** Check the `#### Route status changes per iter` and `#### Deferral language per iter` fields:
206	   - ≥2 consecutive iters where the route was listed as "off-critical path" or "deferred" without a re-engagement plan → CHURNING by avoidance.
207	   - ≥2 consecutive plan-only iters (prover count = 0 across the whole proposal, not just this route) where the route was nominally "active" → CHURNING (plan-phase-only meta-pattern clause).
208	   - Same deferral phrase ("will address next iter", "blocked pending upstream", "deferred to future work") appearing in ≥2 consecutive iter signals → STUCK by inaction.
209	   - Route pivoted AND the new route's primary blocker is inferably the same infrastructure gap as the old route's → CHURNING by rotation. (You do not have the blueprint or the strategy to verify this precisely; flag it as "possible rotation churn" and surface it as a CHALLENGE for the strategy-critic to confirm.)
210	
211	7. **PROGRESS.md dispatch sanity.** Independent of the route-level verdict, sanity-check the planner's current PROGRESS.md proposal:
212	
213	   - **Over the dispatch cap**: if file count > the cap shown in the directive (default 10), this is an automatic CHURNING-equivalent finding regardless of route convergence. Runaway fan-out (e.g. 27 provers in one iter) is the failure mode this check exists to prevent.
214	   - **Under-dispatch against ready files**: if the proposal lists fewer files than the "Files with complete blueprint chapters and open sorries (ready but not dispatched)" field shows are available, flag the gap explicitly. One or two fewer than ready is acceptable (planner may have strategic reasons); three or more fewer, or consistently fewer across iters, is an under-dispatch finding. Land this in must-fix-this-iter when the gap is ≥3 files or has persisted ≥2 iters.
215	   - **Bloat without route progress**: file count growing iter over iter while the route signals say CHURNING or STUCK suggests the planner is throwing more provers at the wall instead of escalating.
216	
217	   These are dispatch-level checks, not route-level. They land in a separate "PROGRESS.md dispatch sanity" block in your report.
218	
219	8. **Throughput honesty.** Compare `Iters left` (verbatim from STRATEGY.md's `## Phases & estimations`) against elapsed iters in the current phase. Bucket:
220	
221	   - **On schedule**: elapsed ≤ estimate.
222	   - **Slipping**: elapsed > estimate but ≤ 2× estimate.
223	   - **Over budget**: elapsed > 2× estimate.
224	   - **Estimate-free**: STRATEGY.md gives no number, or "?", for this row.
225	
226	   "Over budget" with a still-positive `Iters left` is the dishonest-estimate signature. "Estimate-free" with elapsed > 5 iters in a phase is also flag-worthy.
227	
228	## Verdict rules
229	
230	Apply these rules verbatim:
231	
232	- **CONVERGING**: sorry count strictly decreasing in K-iter window AND no recurring blocker AND no under-dispatch pattern AND no avoidance pattern AND planner's proposal looks like "finish what's started."
233	- **CHURNING**: any of the following:
234	  - helpers added in ≥2 of last K iters AND sorry count net unchanged or down by <1 per 2 iters AND no structural change in approach;
235	  - PARTIAL prover status ≥3 of last K iters;
236	  - **plan-phase-only meta-pattern**: ≥3 consecutive iters with zero prover dispatches on this route (no file ever appearing in `## Current Objectives`). Pure planning rounds without ever firing a prover is textbook stall.
237	  - **under-dispatch pattern**: prover count = 1 (or otherwise N < M ready) for ≥2 consecutive iters with no sorry-trajectory improvement attributable to the dispatch strategy (i.e. the filed sorry on the dispatched lane didn't close either).
238	  - **avoidance pattern**: ≥2 consecutive iters with "off-critical path" / "deferred" route status AND no re-engagement plan in the proposal.
239	- **STUCK**: sorry count unchanged across K iters AND prover statuses include INCOMPLETE OR recurring blocker phrase across ≥3 iters. OR: helpers added without any sorry-elimination across K iters. OR: same deferral phrase persisting across ≥2 consecutive iters.
240	- **UNCLEAR**: route is fresh (< K iters of data) OR signals are ambiguous.
241	
242	If multiple rules match a route, pick the worse verdict (CHURNING > CONVERGING; STUCK > CHURNING).
243	
244	## Recommended actions per verdict
245	
246	For CHURNING or STUCK, your report names ONE primary corrective TYPE. The planner consults the catalog for the matching subagent.
247	
248	- **Blueprint expansion** — the chapter's proof sketch is likely under-specified; the planner should expand it before more prover work.
249	- **Mathlib analogy consult** — the project may be using a parallel API or wrong predicate; the planner should consult Mathlib-idiom analysis on the route's load-bearing definitions.
250	- **Refactor** — the definition or file structure may be wrong; dispatch a structural subagent before more prover work.
251	- **Route pivot** — the strategic route may be wrong entirely; revise STRATEGY.md and pick a different route, then re-run any strategy critic mid-iter to validate.
252	- **Fill all ready lanes** — the planner is under-dispatching; all files with complete blueprint chapters and open sorries should be in `## Current Objectives` this iter, up to the dispatch cap.
253	- **Address deferred infrastructure** — the route has been marked off-critical-path or deferred; the planner must either write the blueprint chapter and open a prover lane this iter, or explicitly close the route as out-of-scope (which requires updating `## Goal` in STRATEGY.md if the goal depends on it).
254	- **User escalation** — none of the above will work; pause and request user input. Use sparingly — only when no automated corrective will resolve the stall.
255	
256	Pick ONE primary corrective per CHURNING/STUCK route. Multiple are allowed when truly necessary, listed in priority order.
257	
258	## Report format
259	
260	Write your report to `.archon/task_results/progress-critic-<slug>.md` (or the parent-aware path when invoked nested — your invocation prompt names the exact path).
261	
262	**Omit-empty rule.** Only `## Slug`, `## Iteration`, `## Routes audited`, and `## Overall verdict` are required. Omit any section whose right answer is "nothing to report." Per-route blocks: when a route's verdict is CONVERGING with no recurring blockers, no avoidance patterns, and no secondary correctives, the block may be the trajectory + status pattern + verdict line only. Same for dispatch sanity: if dispatch is OK and no under-dispatch finding exists, render as a single line (`Verdict: OK — file count <N> within cap <C>, no under-dispatch`).
263	
264	```markdown
265	# Progress Critic Report
266	
267	## Slug
268	<slug>
269	
270	## Iteration
271	<NNN>
272	
273	## Routes audited
274	
275	For each route in the directive, one block:
276	
277	### Route: <name>
278	
279	- **Sorry trajectory**: <description, e.g. "5 → 5 → 4 → 4 → 4 across iter-100 to 104">
280	- **Helper accumulation**: <description, e.g. "13 helpers added across last 4 iters; 1 sorry closed">
281	- **Prover dispatch pattern**: <e.g. "1 of 3 ready files dispatched for 3 consecutive iters">
282	- **Recurring blockers**: <list or "none">
283	- **Avoidance patterns**: <list or "none" — name each: off-critical-path reclassification, consecutive plan-only iters, persistent deferral language, possible rotation churn>
284	- **Prover status pattern**: <e.g. "PARTIAL, PARTIAL, PARTIAL, PARTIAL">
285	- **Throughput**: ON_SCHEDULE | SLIPPING | OVER_BUDGET | ESTIMATE_FREE — <estimated K iters, elapsed K'>
286	- **Verdict**: CONVERGING | CHURNING | STUCK | UNCLEAR
287	- **Primary corrective** (if CHURNING/STUCK): <one of the actions above, with one paragraph of why>
288	- **Secondary correctives** (if applicable): <list>
289	
290	## PROGRESS.md dispatch sanity
291	
292	Independent of any route verdict.
293	
294	- **File count**: <N> (cap: <C>)
295	- **Ready but not dispatched**: <list of files with complete chapters and open sorries that are NOT in the proposal, or "none identified">
296	- **Over the cap**: yes | no
297	- **Under-dispatch finding**: yes | no — <if yes: gap size, how many consecutive iters>
298	- **Iter-over-iter trend**: <e.g. "1 → 1 → 1; consistently under-dispatching 3 ready files">
299	- **Verdict**: OK | OVER_CAP | UNDER_DISPATCH | BLOAT_WITHOUT_PROGRESS
300	  - OK: dispatch list is within cap, not under-dispatching ready files, and not growing while routes churn.
301	  - OVER_CAP: more files than the cap allows.
302	  - UNDER_DISPATCH: ≥3 ready files absent from the proposal, or consistently fewer than ready across ≥2 iters. Land in must-fix-this-iter.
303	  - BLOAT_WITHOUT_PROGRESS: file count growing iter over iter while route signals say CHURNING/STUCK.
304	
305	## Must-fix-this-iter <!-- omit entire section if no CHURNING/STUCK verdicts AND no OVER_BUDGET/OVER_CAP/UNDER_DISPATCH/BLOAT findings -->
306	
307	Every CHURNING and every STUCK verdict lands here automatically, every OVER_BUDGET throughput finding (with `Iters left > 0`), and every OVER_CAP / UNDER_DISPATCH / BLOAT_WITHOUT_PROGRESS dispatch verdict. Do not under-classify.
308	
309	- Route <name>: <verdict> — primary corrective: <action>. Why: <one line>.
310	- Route <name>: OVER_BUDGET — STRATEGY.md estimates <K> iters, elapsed <K'>. Revise the estimate or escalate.
311	- Route <name>: avoidance pattern — <specific pattern detected>. Primary corrective: <action>.
312	- Dispatch: UNDER_DISPATCH — <N> ready files absent from proposal for <M> consecutive iters. Fill all ready lanes this iter.
313	- Dispatch: OVER_CAP — planner listed <N> files (cap <C>). Re-prioritize; defer <N-C> files.
314	- Dispatch: BLOAT_WITHOUT_PROGRESS — file count <a> → <b> → <c> while routes <X>, <Y> remain CHURNING.
315

111	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — `CombinatorialCech`
112	
113	The constant-coefficient combinatorial core of standard-cover Čech acyclicity
114	(planner step **L3**, `analogies/p3-localisation.md`).  After localising the
115	extended Čech complex at a spanning element `s_r` (planner step L2, via
116	`exact_of_isLocalized_span`), the fixed index `r` becomes globally available, and
117	the alternating Čech coboundary admits the explicit contracting homotopy
118	`h(t)_{i₀…i_p} = t_{r i₀…i_p}` of Stacks
119	`lemma-cech-cohomology-quasi-coherent-trivial`.
120	
121	This section formalises that homotopy and its consequences for the **constant
122	coefficient** complex `Cᵖ = (Fin (p+1) → ι) → M` with alternating coface
123	differential — abstracted away from the localisation/geometry so the purely
124	combinatorial cancellation (`combHomotopy`) and the simplicial identity
125	(`combDifferential_comp`, `d² = 0`) are isolated and reusable.  The remaining
126	work to close `CechAcyclic.affine` is the **L1** categorical bridge identifying
127	the abstract `CechComplex` terms with the away-localisation modules `M_{s_σ}` and
128	its differential with this `combDifferential` (after localising at `s_r`); these
129	lemmas are then fed to `exact_of_isLocalized_span` node by node.
130	
131	These declarations are `private`: they exist only to close `CechAcyclic.affine`
132	in this file.  The intended blueprint home is the `\lean{...}` bundle of
133	`lem:cech_acyclic_affine`. -/
134	
135	namespace CombinatorialCech
136	
137	variable {ι : Type*} {M : Type*} [AddCommGroup M] {n : ℕ}
138	
139	/-- Alternating coface (Čech) differential with constant coefficients in `M`:
140	`Cⁿ = (Fin n → ι) → M ⟶ Cⁿ⁺¹`, `(d t)(σ) = ∑ⱼ (-1)ʲ • t (σ ∘ j.succAbove)`.
141	The argument `σ ∘ j.succAbove` is the `(n)`-tuple obtained from the `(n+1)`-tuple
142	`σ` by deleting the `j`-th index. -/
143	private def combDifferential (t : (Fin n → ι) → M) : (Fin (n + 1) → ι) → M :=
144	  fun σ => ∑ j : Fin (n + 1), (-1 : ℤ) ^ (j : ℕ) • t (σ ∘ j.succAbove)
145	
146	/-- The contracting homotopy of the localised complex: prepend the fixed
147	(globally invertible after localising at `s_r`) index `r`.
148	`(h u)(τ) = u (Fin.cons r τ)`. -/
149	private def combHomotopy (r : ι) (u : (Fin (n + 1) → ι) → M) : (Fin n → ι) → M :=
150	  fun τ => u (Fin.cons r τ)
151	
152	@[simp] private lemma combHomotopy_zero (r : ι) :
153	    combHomotopy (M := M) (n := n) r 0 = 0 := by
154	  funext τ; simp [combHomotopy]
155	
156	/-- Composing `Fin.cons r` with the `(j+1)`-th coface map is `Fin.cons r` of the
157	`j`-th coface map: the bookkeeping identity behind the homotopy computation. -/
158	private lemma cons_comp_succAbove_succ (r : ι) (σ : Fin (n + 1) → ι) (k : Fin (n + 1)) :
159	    (Fin.cons r σ : Fin (n + 2) → ι) ∘ (k.succ).succAbove
160	      = Fin.cons r (σ ∘ k.succAbove) := by
161	  funext l
162	  refine Fin.cases ?_ ?_ l
163	  · simp
164	  · intro i; simp [Fin.succ_succAbove_succ]
165	
166	/-- **Contracting-homotopy identity** (planner L3; Stacks
167	`lemma-cech-cohomology-quasi-coherent-trivial`):
168	`d ∘ h + h ∘ d = id` on `Cⁿ⁺¹`.  This is the alternating-sum cancellation: the
169	`j = 0` term of `h (d t)` is `t`, and the remaining terms cancel against `d (h t)`
170	in pairs of opposite sign. -/
171	private lemma combHomotopy_spec (r : ι) (t : (Fin (n + 1) → ι) → M) :
172	    combDifferential (combHomotopy r t) + combHomotopy r (combDifferential t) = t := by
173	  funext σ
174	  simp only [combDifferential, combHomotopy, Pi.add_apply]
175	  rw [Fin.sum_univ_succ (f := fun j : Fin (n + 2) =>
176	    (-1 : ℤ) ^ (j : ℕ) • t ((Fin.cons r σ : Fin (n + 2) → ι) ∘ j.succAbove))]
177	  have h0 : (Fin.cons r σ : Fin (n + 2) → ι) ∘ (0 : Fin (n + 2)).succAbove = σ := by
178	    funext i; simp
179	  rw [h0]
180	  simp only [Fin.val_zero, pow_zero, one_smul, Fin.val_succ]
181	  rw [add_left_comm, ← Finset.sum_add_distrib]
182	  rw [Finset.sum_eq_zero (fun x _ => by
183	    rw [cons_comp_succAbove_succ, pow_succ, mul_comm, neg_one_mul, neg_smul]; abel), add_zero]
184	
185	/-- Every cocycle is a coboundary in positive degree: if `d t = 0` then
186	`t = d (h t)`.  This is the homological content of `combHomotopy_spec` and is the
187	half (`ker d ⊆ im d`) carrying the geometric input. -/
188	private lemma combDifferential_eq_of_cocycle (r : ι) (t : (Fin (n + 1) → ι) → M)
189	    (ht : combDifferential t = 0) : combDifferential (combHomotopy r t) = t := by
190	  have h := combHomotopy_spec r t
191	  rw [ht, combHomotopy_zero, add_zero] at h
192	  exact h
193	
194	/-- Sign-cancellation behind `d² = 0`: under the index swap
195	`(j, i) ↦ (j.succAbove i, i.predAbove j)` the alternating sign flips. -/
196	private lemma combSign_flip (j : Fin (n + 2)) (i : Fin (n + 1)) :
197	    ((-1 : ℤ) ^ (j : ℕ)) * ((-1) ^ (i : ℕ))
198	      = - (((-1 : ℤ) ^ ((j.succAbove i : Fin (n + 2)) : ℕ))
199	            * ((-1) ^ ((i.predAbove j : Fin (n + 1)) : ℕ))) := by
200	  rcases lt_or_ge (i.castSucc) j with h | h
201	  · rw [Fin.succAbove_of_castSucc_lt _ _ h, Fin.predAbove_of_castSucc_lt _ _ h,
202	        Fin.val_castSucc, Fin.val_pred]
203	    have hpos : 0 < (j : ℕ) := lt_of_le_of_lt (Nat.zero_le _) (by exact_mod_cast h)
204	    obtain ⟨m, hm⟩ : ∃ m, (j : ℕ) = m + 1 := ⟨(j : ℕ) - 1, by omega⟩
205	    rw [hm]; simp only [Nat.add_sub_cancel, pow_succ]; ring
206	  · rw [Fin.succAbove_of_le_castSucc _ _ h, Fin.predAbove_of_le_castSucc _ _ h,
207	        Fin.val_succ, Fin.coe_castPred]
208	    rw [pow_succ]; ring
209	
210	/-- **`d² = 0`** for the constant-coefficient alternating Čech complex.  Proved by
211	the standard sign-reversing involution `(j, i) ↦ (j.succAbove i, i.predAbove j)` on
212	the double sum: the swap fixes the underlying composite coface
213	(`Fin.succAbove_succAbove_succAbove_predAbove`), is an involution
214	(`Fin.succAbove_succAbove_predAbove`, `Fin.predAbove_predAbove_succAbove`), has no
215	fixed point (`Fin.succAbove_ne`), and flips the sign (`combSign_flip`). -/
216	private lemma combDifferential_comp (t : (Fin n → ι) → M) :
217	    combDifferential (combDifferential t) = 0 := by
218	  funext σ
219	  simp only [combDifferential, Pi.zero_apply, Finset.smul_sum, smul_smul]
220	  rw [← Fintype.sum_prod_type (f := fun p : Fin (n + 2) × Fin (n + 1) =>
221	    ((-1 : ℤ) ^ (p.1 : ℕ) * (-1) ^ (p.2 : ℕ)) • t ((σ ∘ p.1.succAbove) ∘ p.2.succAbove))]
222	  apply Finset.sum_involution (fun p _ => (p.1.succAbove p.2, p.2.predAbove p.1))
223	  · rintro ⟨j, i⟩ _
224	    have harg : (σ ∘ (j.succAbove i).succAbove) ∘ (i.predAbove j).succAbove
225	        = (σ ∘ j.succAbove) ∘ i.succAbove := by
226	      funext k
227	      simp only [Function.comp_apply]
228	      rw [Fin.succAbove_succAbove_succAbove_predAbove]
229	    simp only [harg]
230	    rw [← add_smul, combSign_flip j i]
231	    simp
232	  · rintro ⟨j, i⟩ _ _
233	    simp only [ne_eq, Prod.mk.injEq, not_and]
234	    intro hj
235	    exact absurd hj (Fin.succAbove_ne j i)
236	  · rintro ⟨j, i⟩ _
237	    simp only [Prod.mk.injEq]
238	    exact ⟨Fin.succAbove_succAbove_predAbove j i, Fin.predAbove_predAbove_succAbove j i⟩
239	  · intro a _; exact Finset.mem_univ _
240	
241	/-- **Positive-degree exactness** of the constant-coefficient Čech complex in the
242	`Function.Exact` form that `exact_of_isLocalized_span` (planner L2) consumes node
243	by node.  Combines `combDifferential_comp` (`im ⊆ ker`) with
244	`combDifferential_eq_of_cocycle` (`ker ⊆ im`, the homotopy half).  Requires a
245	distinguished index `r : ι` — supplied, after localising at `s_r`, by the
246	spanning element itself. -/
247	private lemma combDifferential_exact (r : ι) (n : ℕ) :
248	    Function.Exact (combDifferential : ((Fin (n + 1) → ι) → M) → ((Fin (n + 2) → ι) → M))
249	      (combDifferential : ((Fin (n + 2) → ι) → M) → ((Fin (n + 3) → ι) → M)) := by
250	  intro x
251	  constructor
252	  · intro hx
253	    exact ⟨combHomotopy r x, combDifferential_eq_of_cocycle r x hx⟩
254	  · rintro ⟨y, rfl⟩
255	    exact combDifferential_comp y
256	
257	/-! ### Dependent-coefficient port (planner **L3 port**)
258	
259	The localised complex that `exact_of_isLocalized_span` (planner **L2**) consumes
260	node-by-node has *varying* coefficients `M_{s_σ}` — the away localisation of `M`
261	at the product `s_σ = ∏ₖ s_{σ k}` — not a single constant module `M`.  After
262	localising the whole extended complex at a spanning element `s_r`, the index `r`
263	is globally available and `s_r` becomes a unit, so the prepend
264	`σ ↦ Fin.cons r σ` is an *isomorphism* on the coefficients:
265	`M_{s_{cons r σ}} = M_{s_r · s_σ} ≅ M_{s_σ}` because `s_r` is already invertible.
266	
267	This section ports the constant-coefficient cancellation
268	(`combHomotopy_spec`, `combDifferential_eq_of_cocycle`) to that dependent
269	setting.  The away-localisation restriction maps are abstracted as additive
270	coface maps `δ` (`M_{s_{σ∘dⱼ}} → M_{s_σ}`, well defined because `s_σ` carries the
271	extra factor `s_{σ j}`) and the prepend isomorphisms as additive maps `c`
272	(`M_{s_{cons r σ}} → M_{s_σ}`).  The two compatibility identities below are
273	exactly the ones the away-localisation maps satisfy:
274	
275	* the **unit** identity `c ∘ δ₀ = id` (deleting the prepended `r` from `cons r σ`
276	  recovers `σ`; on localisations the composite restriction is the identity since
277	  `s_r` is a unit), and
278	* the **shift** identity `c ∘ δ_{k+1} = δ_k ∘ c` (prepend commutes with the later
279	  cofaces), which is `cons_comp_succAbove_succ` lifted to the localisation maps.
280	
281	Threading the dependent family through the same alternating-sum cancellation as
282	the constant case then yields the dependent homotopy identity and the
283	cocycle⟹coboundary corollary that L2 consumes.  Constructing the actual `δ`/`c`
284	from `IsLocalizedModule.Away` (Mathlib `tilde` API:
285	`AlgebraicGeometry.Modules.Tilde`, `IsLocalizedModule (.powers f)`) and the L1
286	identification of the abstract `CechComplex` terms with `∏_σ M_{s_σ}` remain the
287	outstanding bridge (see `CechAcyclic.affine`). -/
288	
289	section Dependent
290	
291	variable {A : (m : ℕ) → (Fin m → ι) → Type*} [∀ m σ, AddCommGroup (A m σ)]
292	variable (r : ι)
293	variable (δ : (m : ℕ) → (σ : Fin (m + 1) → ι) → (j : Fin (m + 1)) →
294	    A m (σ ∘ j.succAbove) →+ A (m + 1) σ)
295	variable (c : (m : ℕ) → (σ : Fin m → ι) → A (m + 1) (Fin.cons r σ) →+ A m σ)
296	
297	omit [∀ m σ, AddCommGroup (A m σ)] in
298	/-- Transport of a dependent cochain value along an equality of index tuples.
299	The transport friction that the constant-coefficient proof avoided (there every
300	coefficient is the same `M`) is isolated here. -/
301	private lemma depTransport {m : ℕ} {x y : Fin m → ι} (h : x = y)
302	    (t : ∀ σ : Fin m → ι, A m σ) : h ▸ t x = t y := by
303	  subst h; rfl
304	
305	/-- Deleting the prepended index `r` (the `0`-th coface of `Fin.cons r σ`)
306	recovers `σ`. -/
307	private lemma cons_comp_zero_succAbove {m : ℕ} (σ : Fin m → ι) :
308	    (Fin.cons r σ : Fin (m + 1) → ι) ∘ (0 : Fin (m + 1)).succAbove = σ := by
309	  funext i; simp
310	
311	/-- Dependent (varying-coefficient) alternating Čech differential built from the
312	coface (localisation restriction) maps `δ`. -/
313	private def depDiff {m : ℕ} (t : ∀ σ : Fin m → ι, A m σ) :
314	    ∀ σ : Fin (m + 1) → ι, A (m + 1) σ :=
315	  fun σ => ∑ j : Fin (m + 1), (-1 : ℤ) ^ (j : ℕ) • δ m σ j (t (σ ∘ j.succAbove))
316	
317	/-- Dependent contracting homotopy: prepend the distinguished index `r`, then
318	apply the prepend map `c`. -/
319	private def depHomotopy {m : ℕ} (u : ∀ σ : Fin (m + 1) → ι, A (m + 1) σ) :
320	    ∀ σ : Fin m → ι, A m σ :=
321	  fun σ => c m σ (u (Fin.cons r σ))
322	
323	/-- **Dependent contracting-homotopy identity** (planner L3 port): `d ∘ h + h ∘ d
324	= id`, evaluated at a tuple `σ`.  Same alternating-sum cancellation as
325	`combHomotopy_spec`, threaded through the varying coefficients via the unit
326	identity `hu` (`c ∘ δ₀ = id`) and the shift identity `hsh`
327	(`c ∘ δ_{k+1} = δ_k ∘ c`). -/
328	private lemma depHomotopy_spec
329	    (hu : ∀ {m : ℕ} (σ : Fin (m + 1) → ι)
330	        (y : A (m + 1)
331	          ((Fin.cons r σ : Fin (m + 2) → ι) ∘ (0 : Fin (m + 2)).succAbove)),
332	        c (m + 1) σ (δ (m + 1) (Fin.cons r σ) 0 y)
333	          = (cons_comp_zero_succAbove r σ) ▸ y)
334	    (hsh : ∀ {m : ℕ} (σ : Fin (m + 1) → ι) (k : Fin (m + 1))
335	        (y : A (m + 1)
336	          ((Fin.cons r σ : Fin (m + 2) → ι) ∘ (k.succ).succAbove)),
337	        c (m + 1) σ (δ (m + 1) (Fin.cons r σ) (k.succ) y)
338	          = δ m σ k (c m (σ ∘ k.succAbove) ((cons_comp_succAbove_succ r σ k) ▸ y)))
339	    {m : ℕ} (t : ∀ σ : Fin (m + 1) → ι, A (m + 1) σ) (σ : Fin (m + 1) → ι) :
340	    depDiff δ (depHomotopy r c t) σ + depHomotopy r c (depDiff δ t) σ = t σ := by
341	  simp only [depDiff, depHomotopy]
342	  rw [map_sum]
343	  simp only [map_zsmul]
344	  rw [Fin.sum_univ_succ (f := fun k : Fin (m + 2) =>
345	    (-1 : ℤ) ^ (k : ℕ) • c (m + 1) σ
346	      (δ (m + 1) (Fin.cons r σ) k (t ((Fin.cons r σ : Fin (m + 2) → ι) ∘ k.succAbove))))]
347	  -- the `k = 0` term collapses to `t σ` via the unit identity
348	  rw [hu σ (t _)]
349	  rw [depTransport (cons_comp_zero_succAbove r σ) t]
350	  simp only [Fin.val_zero, pow_zero, one_smul, Fin.val_succ]
351	  -- the `k = j.succ` terms cancel against the `d ∘ h` sum in pairs of opposite sign
352	  rw [add_left_comm, ← Finset.sum_add_distrib]
353	  rw [Finset.sum_eq_zero (fun x _ => by
354	    rw [hsh σ x (t _), depTransport (cons_comp_succAbove_succ r σ x) t,
355	      pow_succ, mul_comm, neg_one_mul, neg_smul]
356	    abel), add_zero]
357	
358	/-- Dependent cocycle⟹coboundary (the geometric half consumed by L2): if
359	`depDiff t = 0` then `t = depDiff (depHomotopy t)`. -/
360	private lemma depDiff_eq_of_cocycle
361	    (hu : ∀ {m : ℕ} (σ : Fin (m + 1) → ι)
362	        (y : A (m + 1)
363	          ((Fin.cons r σ : Fin (m + 2) → ι) ∘ (0 : Fin (m + 2)).succAbove)),
364	        c (m + 1) σ (δ (m + 1) (Fin.cons r σ) 0 y)
365	          = (cons_comp_zero_succAbove r σ) ▸ y)
366	    (hsh : ∀ {m : ℕ} (σ : Fin (m + 1) → ι) (k : Fin (m + 1))
367	        (y : A (m + 1)
368	          ((Fin.cons r σ : Fin (m + 2) → ι) ∘ (k.succ).succAbove)),
369	        c (m + 1) σ (δ (m + 1) (Fin.cons r σ) (k.succ) y)
370	          = δ m σ k (c m (σ ∘ k.succAbove) ((cons_comp_succAbove_succ r σ k) ▸ y)))
371	    {m : ℕ} (t : ∀ σ : Fin (m + 1) → ι, A (m + 1) σ)
372	    (ht : depDiff δ t = 0) (σ : Fin (m + 1) → ι) :
373	    depDiff δ (depHomotopy r c t) σ = t σ := by
374	  have h := depHomotopy_spec r δ c hu hsh t σ
375	  rw [show depHomotopy r c (depDiff δ t) σ = 0 by rw [ht]; simp [depHomotopy], add_zero] at h
376	  exact h
377	
378	omit [∀ m σ, AddCommGroup (A m σ)] in
379	/-- The composite coface tuple is symmetric under the `d²=0` index swap
380	`(j, i) ↦ (j.succAbove i, i.predAbove j)` (dependent analogue of the `harg` step
381	inside `combDifferential_comp`). -/
382	private lemma comp_succAbove_swap {m : ℕ} (σ : Fin (m + 2) → ι)
383	    (j : Fin (m + 2)) (i : Fin (m + 1)) :
384	    (σ ∘ (j.succAbove i).succAbove) ∘ (i.predAbove j).succAbove
385	      = (σ ∘ j.succAbove) ∘ i.succAbove := by
386	  funext k
387	  simp only [Function.comp_apply]
388	  rw [Fin.succAbove_succAbove_succAbove_predAbove]
389	
390	/-- **Dependent `d² = 0`** for the varying-coefficient alternating Čech complex.
391	Same sign-reversing involution `(j, i) ↦ (j.succAbove i, i.predAbove j)` as
392	`combDifferential_comp`, with the coefficient transport handled by
393	`comp_succAbove_swap` and the coface-commutation hypothesis `hcomm` (the two
394	restriction maps into `M_{s_σ}` agree — a formal property of localisation maps,
395	independent of the choice of `r`). -/
396	private lemma depDiff_comp
397	    (hcomm : ∀ {m : ℕ} (σ : Fin (m + 2) → ι) (j : Fin (m + 2)) (i : Fin (m + 1))
398	        (z : A m ((σ ∘ j.succAbove) ∘ i.succAbove)),
399	        δ (m + 1) σ j (δ m (σ ∘ j.succAbove) i z)
400	          = δ (m + 1) σ (j.succAbove i)
401	              (δ m (σ ∘ (j.succAbove i).succAbove) (i.predAbove j)
402	                ((comp_succAbove_swap σ j i).symm ▸ z)))
403	    {m : ℕ} (t : ∀ σ : Fin m → ι, A m σ) :
404	    depDiff δ (depDiff δ t) = 0 := by
405	  funext σ
406	  simp only [depDiff, Pi.zero_apply, map_sum, map_zsmul, Finset.smul_sum, smul_smul]
407	  rw [← Fintype.sum_prod_type (f := fun p : Fin (m + 2) × Fin (m + 1) =>
408	    ((-1 : ℤ) ^ (p.1 : ℕ) * (-1) ^ (p.2 : ℕ)) •
409	      δ (m + 1) σ p.1 (δ m (σ ∘ p.1.succAbove) p.2 (t ((σ ∘ p.1.succAbove) ∘ p.2.succAbove))))]
410	  apply Finset.sum_involution (fun p _ => (p.1.succAbove p.2, p.2.predAbove p.1))
411	  · rintro ⟨j, i⟩ _
412	    simp only []
413	    rw [hcomm σ j i (t _), depTransport (comp_succAbove_swap σ j i).symm t,
414	      ← add_smul, combSign_flip j i]
415	    simp
416	  · rintro ⟨j, i⟩ _ _
417	    simp only [ne_eq, Prod.mk.injEq, not_and]
418	    intro hj
419	    exact absurd hj (Fin.succAbove_ne j i)
420	  · rintro ⟨j, i⟩ _
421	    simp only [Prod.mk.injEq]
422	    exact ⟨Fin.succAbove_succAbove_predAbove j i, Fin.predAbove_predAbove_succAbove j i⟩
423	  · intro a _; exact Finset.mem_univ _
424	
425	/-- **Dependent positive-degree exactness** in the `Function.Exact` form that
426	`exact_of_isLocalized_span` (planner **L2**) consumes after localising at a
427	spanning element `s_r`.  Combines `depDiff_comp` (`im ⊆ ker`) with
428	`depDiff_eq_of_cocycle` (`ker ⊆ im`, the homotopy half carrying the
429	`s_r`-invertibility input).  This is the dependent-coefficient analogue of
430	`combDifferential_exact`. -/
431	private lemma depDiff_exact
432	    (hu : ∀ {m : ℕ} (σ : Fin (m + 1) → ι)
433	        (y : A (m + 1)
434	          ((Fin.cons r σ : Fin (m + 2) → ι) ∘ (0 : Fin (m + 2)).succAbove)),
435	        c (m + 1) σ (δ (m + 1) (Fin.cons r σ) 0 y)
436	          = (cons_comp_zero_succAbove r σ) ▸ y)
437	    (hsh : ∀ {m : ℕ} (σ : Fin (m + 1) → ι) (k : Fin (m + 1))
438	        (y : A (m + 1)
439	          ((Fin.cons r σ : Fin (m + 2) → ι) ∘ (k.succ).succAbove)),
440	        c (m + 1) σ (δ (m + 1) (Fin.cons r σ) (k.succ) y)
441	          = δ m σ k (c m (σ ∘ k.succAbove) ((cons_comp_succAbove_succ r σ k) ▸ y)))
442	    (hcomm : ∀ {m : ℕ} (σ : Fin (m + 2) → ι) (j : Fin (m + 2)) (i : Fin (m + 1))
443	        (z : A m ((σ ∘ j.succAbove) ∘ i.succAbove)),
444	        δ (m + 1) σ j (δ m (σ ∘ j.succAbove) i z)
445	          = δ (m + 1) σ (j.succAbove i)
446	              (δ m (σ ∘ (j.succAbove i).succAbove) (i.predAbove j)
447	                ((comp_succAbove_swap σ j i).symm ▸ z)))
448	    (m : ℕ) :
449	    Function.Exact (depDiff δ (m := m + 1)) (depDiff δ (m := m + 2)) := by
450	  intro x
451	  constructor
452	  · intro hx
453	    exact ⟨depHomotopy r c x,
454	      funext fun σ => depDiff_eq_of_cocycle r δ c hu hsh x hx σ⟩
455	  · rintro ⟨y, rfl⟩
456	    exact depDiff_comp δ hcomm y
457	
458	end Dependent
459	
460	end CombinatorialCech
461	
462	end AlgebraicGeometry

11	# Free-presheaf Čech complex (P3b bridge — free-complex side)
12	
13	This file is the free-presheaf-complex side of the P3b bridge.  The section side lives in

17	  — the chain complex of free presheaves of `O_X`-modules whose degree-`p` term is
18	  `⨁_{σ : Fin(p+1) → ι} (PresheafOfModules.free X.ringCatSheaf.obj).obj`
19	  `  (yoneda.obj (⨅ k, U (σ k)))`.
20

33	blueprint §Presheaf-level Čech machinery):

35	────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
36	`cechFreePresheafComplex`
37	────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
38	Goal: a `ChainComplex X.PresheafOfModules ℕ` whose degree-`p` term is
39	  `⨁_{σ : Fin(p+1) → ι} (PresheafOfModules.free X.ringCatSheaf.obj).obj (yoneda.obj (⨅ k, U (σ k)))`
40	with the alternating-face differential.
41	
42	Recommended build path — use the simplicial route so that d²=0 comes for free:
43	1. Construct a `SimplicialObject X.PresheafOfModules` whose `n`-simplices are the
44	   above direct sums.
45	2. Apply `AlgebraicTopology.alternatingFaceMapComplex` to obtain the chain complex.
46	   This gives d²=0 automatically via `SimplicialObject.boundarySquareZero`.
47	
48	Key API:
49	- `PresheafOfModules.free X.ringCatSheaf.obj :`
50	  `  ((Opens ↥X)ᵒᵖ ⥤ Type u) ⥤ PresheafOfModules X.ringCatSheaf.obj`
51	  — the free-presheaf-of-modules functor.  Use it as `(PresheafOfModules.free _).obj` and
52	  `(PresheafOfModules.free _).map`.
53	- `yoneda.obj V : (Opens ↥X)ᵒᵖ ⥤ Type u` — the representable presheaf of sets at `V`.
54	  Do NOT introduce a bespoke `j_!`; `free ∘ yoneda` is the correct substitute.
55	- `AlgebraicTopology.alternatingFaceMapComplex` — turns a simplicial abelian group (or
56	  simplicial object in an abelian category) into a chain complex; d²=0 is a theorem.
57	- Direct sums: `⨁` is `DirectSum`; in `PresheafOfModules` use
58	  `Limits.biproduct` / `DirectSum.lof` / `DirectSum.desc`.

61	path above.
62	
63	────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
64	`cechFreeComplex_quasiIso`
65	────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
66	Goal: show that `cechFreePresheafComplex` → `O_𝒰[0]` is a quasi-isomorphism (i.e.,
67	the free complex is a free resolution of the structure sheaf restricted to the cover).
68	
69	Recommended build path — objectwise contracting homotopy:
70	1. Homology in `X.PresheafOfModules` is computed objectwise (colimits are objectwise).
71	   So it suffices to exhibit, for each open `V : Opens ↥X`, a contracting homotopy on
72	   the sectionwise complex `cechFreePresheafComplex(V)`.
73	2. Sectionwise at `V`, degree `p` is `⨁_{σ : Fin(p+1) → ι} R(V)` for those multi-indices

88	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — free-presheaf Čech complex
89	
90	This section builds the free-presheaf Čech complex `cechFreePresheafComplex` of a finite
91	open cover `𝒰 : X.OpenCover` as the alternating-face-map chain complex of an explicit
92	simplicial object `cechFreeSimplicial`.  Everything is project-local: Mathlib has the
93	free-presheaf-of-modules functor, the Yoneda embedding and `alternatingFaceMapComplex`,
94	but not their assembly into the Čech free resolution of a cover.
95	
96	We require `[Finite 𝒰.I₀]` so that the index types `Fin (p+1) → 𝒰.I₀` of the degreewise
97	coproducts are finite (`HasCoproductsOfShape`); this matches the finiteness hypothesis of
98	the downstream protected theorem `cech_computes_higherDirectImage`. -/
99	
100	variable {X : Scheme.{u}}
101	
102	/-- **Free presheaf of modules on a representable open.**
103	
104	The composite `Opens X --yoneda--> ((Opens X)ᵒᵖ ⥤ Type) --free--> X.PresheafOfModules`.
105	For an open `V`, `freeYoneda.obj V` is the extension-by-zero free presheaf of modules
106	`(j_V)_! O_X|_V` of the blueprint: on sections over `W` it is the free `O_X(W)`-module on
107	`Hom(W, V)`, i.e. `O_X(W)` if `W ⊆ V` and `0` otherwise.
108	
109	Project-local because Mathlib provides `PresheafOfModules.free` and `yoneda` separately but
110	not this composite, which is the basic building block of the free Čech complex. -/
111	noncomputable def freeYoneda : TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥X ⥤ X.PresheafOfModules :=
112	  yoneda ⋙ PresheafOfModules.free X.ringCatSheaf.obj
113	
114	/-- The open underlying the `i`-th member of an open cover, as `(𝒰.f i).opensRange`. -/
115	def coverOpen (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) (i : 𝒰.I₀) : TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥X := (𝒰.f i).opensRange
116	
117	/-- The intersection open `⨅ k, U (σ k)` indexed by a tuple `σ : κ → 𝒰.I₀`.
118	
119	For `κ = Fin (p+1)` this is the `(p+1)`-fold intersection `U_{σ(0)…σ(p)}` indexing the
120	degree-`p` term of the Čech complex. -/
121	def coverInterOpen (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) {κ : Type} (σ : κ → 𝒰.I₀) : TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥X :=
122	  ⨅ k, coverOpen 𝒰 (σ k)
123	
124	/-- Reindexing along `α : κ' → κ` enlarges the intersection open: precomposing a tuple with
125	`α` keeps a subset of the indices, so the intersection grows. This `≤` supplies the
126	representable index-dropping maps that define the Čech differential. -/
127	lemma coverInterOpen_comp_le (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) {κ κ' : Type} (α : κ' → κ) (σ : κ → 𝒰.I₀) :
128	    coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ ≤ coverInterOpen 𝒰 (σ ∘ α) :=
129	  le_iInf fun k => iInf_le _ (α k)
130	
131	/-- A coproduct injection transported along an equality of indices: `eqToHom` of the induced
132	object equality cancels the index change. A generic categorical helper used to discharge the
133	dependent-index bookkeeping in the simplicial identities of `cechFreeSimplicial`. -/
134	private lemma sigma_ι_eqToHom_transport {C : Type*} [Category C] {β : Type*} (B : β → C)
135	    [HasCoproduct B] {a b : β} (e : a = b) :
136	    eqToHom (congrArg B e) ≫ Limits.Sigma.ι B b = Limits.Sigma.ι B a := by
137	  subst e; simp
138	
139	/-- **The free Čech simplicial object of a finite open cover.**
140	
141	The simplicial object in `X.PresheafOfModules` whose degree-`p` term is the coproduct
142	`∐_{σ : Fin (p+1) → 𝒰.I₀} freeYoneda.obj (coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ)` and whose simplicial maps
143	reindex the multi-index `σ ↦ σ ∘ α` (along the order map of `α : [p] ⟶ [q]`) together with
144	the representable index-dropping inclusion `coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ ≤ coverInterOpen 𝒰 (σ ∘ α)`.
145	
146	Taking its `alternatingFaceMapComplex` produces `cechFreePresheafComplex` with `d² = 0` for
147	free (the simplicial route avoids hand-rolling the alternating-sum identity).
148	
149	Project-local: this is the simplicial backbone of the free Čech resolution; it has no
150	Mathlib counterpart. -/
151	noncomputable def cechFreeSimplicial (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) [Finite 𝒰.I₀] :
152	    SimplicialObject X.PresheafOfModules where
153	  obj n := ∐ fun σ : Fin (n.unop.len + 1) → 𝒰.I₀ => freeYoneda.obj (coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ)
154	  map {n m} α := Limits.Sigma.desc fun σ =>
155	    freeYoneda.map (homOfLE (coverInterOpen_comp_le 𝒰 α.unop.toOrderHom σ)) ≫
156	      Limits.Sigma.ι (fun τ : Fin (m.unop.len + 1) → 𝒰.I₀ => freeYoneda.obj (coverInterOpen 𝒰 τ))
157	        (σ ∘ α.unop.toOrderHom)
158	  map_id n := by
159	    apply Limits.Sigma.hom_ext; intro σ
160	    simp only [Limits.Sigma.ι_desc, Category.comp_id]
161	    have e : σ ∘ ⇑(SimplexCategory.Hom.toOrderHom (𝟙 n).unop) = σ := by funext i; simp
162	    have eo : coverInterOpen 𝒰 (σ ∘ ⇑(SimplexCategory.Hom.toOrderHom (𝟙 n).unop))
163	        = coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ := by rw [e]
164	    rw [Subsingleton.elim (homOfLE
165	        (coverInterOpen_comp_le 𝒰 (SimplexCategory.Hom.toOrderHom (𝟙 n).unop) σ)) (eqToHom eo.symm),
166	      eqToHom_map]
167	    exact sigma_ι_eqToHom_transport
168	      (fun σ : Fin (n.unop.len + 1) → 𝒰.I₀ => freeYoneda.obj (coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ)) e
169	  map_comp {n m k} f g := by
170	    apply Limits.Sigma.hom_ext; intro σ
171	    simp only [Category.assoc, Limits.Sigma.ι_desc, Limits.Sigma.ι_desc_assoc]
172	    have e : σ ∘ ⇑(SimplexCategory.Hom.toOrderHom (f ≫ g).unop)
173	        = (σ ∘ ⇑(SimplexCategory.Hom.toOrderHom f.unop))
174	            ∘ ⇑(SimplexCategory.Hom.toOrderHom g.unop) := by
175	      funext i; simp [unop_comp, SimplexCategory.comp_toOrderHom]
176	    rw [← sigma_ι_eqToHom_transport
177	          (fun τ : Fin (k.unop.len + 1) → 𝒰.I₀ => freeYoneda.obj (coverInterOpen 𝒰 τ)) e,
178	      show (eqToHom (congrArg (fun τ : Fin (k.unop.len + 1) → 𝒰.I₀ =>
179	              freeYoneda.obj (coverInterOpen 𝒰 τ)) e))
180	          = freeYoneda.map (eqToHom (congrArg (coverInterOpen 𝒰) e)) from (eqToHom_map _ _).symm]
181	    simp only [← Category.assoc, ← Functor.map_comp]
182	    congr 2
183	
184	/-- **The free-presheaf Čech complex of a finite open cover** (`def:cech_free_presheaf_complex`).
185	
186	The chain complex `K(𝒰)_•` in `X.PresheafOfModules` whose degree-`p` term is
187	`∐_{σ : Fin (p+1) → 𝒰.I₀} freeYoneda.obj (coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ)`
188	`= ⨁_σ (PresheafOfModules.free _).obj (yoneda.obj (⨅ k, U (σ k)))`, with the
189	alternating-sum index-dropping differential. Built as the `alternatingFaceMapComplex` of
190	`cechFreeSimplicial`, so `d² = 0` holds by the simplicial identities.
191	
192	Project-local: the free Čech resolution of a cover is not in Mathlib. -/
193	noncomputable def cechFreePresheafComplex (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) [Finite 𝒰.I₀] :
194	    ChainComplex X.PresheafOfModules ℕ :=
195	  (AlgebraicTopology.alternatingFaceMapComplex X.PresheafOfModules).obj (cechFreeSimplicial 𝒰)
196	
197	/-- Degreewise unfolding of the free Čech complex: the degree-`p` term is the coproduct of
198	free presheaves over the `(p+1)`-fold multi-indices. Exposed (it holds by `rfl`) so that the
199	downstream hom-identification and quasi-isomorphism arguments can rewrite the degree-`p`
200	object without unfolding `alternatingFaceMapComplex` and `cechFreeSimplicial` by hand. -/
201	lemma cechFreePresheafComplex_X (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) [Finite 𝒰.I₀] (p : ℕ) :
202	    (cechFreePresheafComplex 𝒰).X p
203	      = ∐ fun σ : Fin (p + 1) → 𝒰.I₀ => freeYoneda.obj (coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ) :=
204	  rfl
205	
206	end AlgebraicGeometry
